title: Roundup Fast Action Weedkiller, Ready to Use, Manual Spray, 3 Litre
features: Fast action ready to use weed killer that kills the weeds and roots with visible results in 1-2 days
Kills most garden weeds with a single application; up to 3 minutes of continuous spray when using 3 L option
Kills the roots so weeds don't come back
Children and pets need not be excluded from treated areas (once dry)
Degraded in the soil by micro organisms
Leaves no harmful residues to allow replanting within 7 days

Kills right to the roots Travels through the leaves straight to the root to prevent weeds from growing back or regenerating. Perfect for paths, drives, patios and flower beds Use all over the garden on paths, drives, patios and flowerbeds to keep them free from pesky weeds. Degrades naturally in soil to allow replanting Degrades naturally in soil leaving no residue, meaning you can start replanting in just 7 days. Next page 1 Results In 48 Hours 2 Kills To The Root 3 Paths, Drives, Flowerbeds 4 Replant In 7 Days Fast and effective weed control that gets to work within hours of application Ready to use straight from the bottle, this premixed glyphosate formula moves through weeds all the way to the roots and is naturally broken down within a week by micro-organisms in the soil, so you can get planting again. Our Pump 'N Go sprayer offers a one touch trigger system and an extendable wand - it gets you closer to weeds without bending, so you can remove weeds faster and easier than ever - 3 mins of continuous spray with the 2.5 litre pack, and 10 mins in the 5 litre pack. Why should I use ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller? A multi-purpose weedkiller, ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller kills annual and deep rooted perennial weeds, including grasses, dandelions, docks, nettles, ground elder, bindweed and thistles. It is inactivated on contact with soil. Where can I use ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller? Approved for use all around the garden, on gravel areas, paths, drives and along fences. ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready to Use Weedkiller can be used to clean up weedy areas before planting vegetables, flowers, shrubs and to renovate overgrown areas. When should I use ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller? Spray any time when weeds are actively growing and have adequate leaf area to absorb spray. Apply in calm conditions to avoid drift onto cultivated plants. Rain within 6 hours of spraying reduces effectiveness and repeat treatments may be necessary. How do I use ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller? Apply spray evenly to wet the leaves of the weeds, avoiding run-off. Leave weeds 7 days before digging or soil cultivation, for the weedkiller to move to the roots. Repeat application may be necessary, should new growth occur. Is ROUNDUP Fast Action Ready To Use Weedkiller safe to use on areas used by children and pets? Keep children and pets away from treated areas until spray has dried. Keep out of the reach of children. product_details_tab_data.Safety Warning: Use weedkillers safely. Always read the label. Keep children and pets out of treated areas until dry. Although not a requirement, as good practice, we recommend wearing waterproof gloves and gardening footwear treat established perennial weeds at the start of flowering to give best results. Spray nettles and ground elder when new leaves have unfolded fully in late spring. Weeds may show first effects from a few days up to 4 weeks, depending on the weather and the type of weed. Leave weeds 7 days before digging or soil cultivation, for the weedkiller to move to the roots. Repeat application if necessary, should new growth appear. This product is a home garden weedkiller for use on unwanted vegetation and areas not intended to bear vegetation. It contains 7.2g/l glyphosate acid (present as 9.7g/1) ipa salt of glyphosate in a ready to use solution. Risk and safety information read label before use keep out of reach of children keep product away from food and drink if medical advice is needed, have product container or label at hand keep product in original container dispose of contents/container to a household waste recycling centre as hazardous waste except for empty containers which can be disposed of by recycling. Contact your local council for details. To avoid risks to human health and the environment, comply with the instructions for use. Keep children and pets away from treated areas until spray has dried child safe trigger protect from frost
